title: T8: SessionStart revision_hash gating + cross-client visibility verification
description: Q-14 resolved: SessionStart dumping full broad list every session causes banner blindness. Solution: gate the dump on revision_hash change. (1) `.fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash` stores the last-emitted revision_hash. SessionStart hook reads it; if matches current revision_hash → silent exit 0 (no re-dump). Any knowledge change → revision_hash differs → emit banner + update sidecar. (2) Q-15 resolved: cross-client stderr visibility unverified. Run manual verification round in Claude Code / Cursor (with `followup_message` field) / Codex CLI; document where stderr renders in each UI with screenshots in new `docs/cross-client-visibility.md`.

files.change: add revision_hash gating: compute current hash from canonical/ directory contents (existing helper from rc.5), read `.fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash`, compare; if equal → exit 0 silently; if differ → emit banner AND write current hash to sidecar
(may already exist from rc.5) ensure `computeRevisionHash()` is exported and stable across calls for unchanged canonical/
test: first run writes sidecar + emits; immediate second run reads sidecar + silent exit; canonical change → new hash → emit + sidecar update

create new doc: per-client (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI) section documenting where stderr from SessionStart and Stop hooks renders; include screenshots of each; document `followup_message` JSON field behavior in Cursor; note any known divergence
add check: SessionStart hook with unchanged revision_hash emits no stderr; with changed revision_hash emits banner and updates sidecar

implementation: Identify or implement computeRevisionHash() — hash canonical/ directory file list + mtimes (or content checksum); must be deterministic and fast (<100ms)

Wire revision_hash gating into knowledge-hint-broad.cjs: read sidecar (.fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash); if equal to current hash → silent exit 0; otherwise emit banner + atomically write new hash to sidecar
Run manual verification round: in Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI — start a session, force SessionStart hook with revision change, capture screenshots of stderr rendering location

Write docs/cross-client-visibility.md: per-client section with screenshot + 1-paragraph description of UX (e.g., 'Cursor: stderr appears as an inline tool result block above the input'); document followup_message JSON behavior in Cursor
Update unit tests to cover gating logic

reference.pattern: rc.5 revision_hash mechanism (if exists); .fabric/.cache/ sidecar pattern
reference.examples: Sidecar single-line text file holding hex hash

criteria: GIVEN canonical/ unchanged across two sessions WHEN SessionStart hook fires the second session THEN hook exits 0 with empty stderr AND sidecar unchanged
GIVEN canonical/ changed (new entry added) since last session WHEN SessionStart hook fires THEN stderr contains the banner AND `.fabric/.cache/sessionstart-last-hash` is updated to the new hash
docs/cross-client-visibility.md exists with sections for Claude Code, Cursor, Codex CLI — each with a screenshot or rendered example showing where SessionStart stderr appears in the UI
scripts/rc7-coverage-gate.mjs assertion: simulate unchanged revision → no stderr; simulate changed revision → stderr present + sidecar updated

rationale.chosen_approach: Hash-of-canonical-dir + sidecar gating — same primitive as rc.5 revision_hash for `fab_plan_context`, reused at hook level
alternatives_considered: Always dump but with diff-only output
decision_factors: Q-14 problem is 'same content shown every session'; hash equivalence is the exact predicate
rationale.tradeoffs: If user wants to see canonical reminder without canonical change (e.g., 'remind me what I have'), gating hides it — accepted, `fab_get_knowledge_sections` is the on-demand surface

manual_checks: Verify in Claude Code: open project, screenshot SessionStart stderr location
Add one canonical entry; reopen session; verify banner reappears
success_metrics: First-session emit + sidecar write: 100% of cold-start runs
Cross-client doc has visibility-verified screenshots for all 3 clients
unit: Revision hash deterministic for identical canonical/ inputs
integration: Simulate 3 sessions: first emits, second silent, third (after canonical add) emits again
